title: MeshBody Object
description: Provides access to a mesh body.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: copy
methods.description: Copies the mesh body to the clipboard.

methods.name: copyToComponent
methods.description: Creates a copy of this mesh body into the specified target.

methods.name: createComponent
methods.description: Creates a new component and occurrence within the component that currently owns this body. This body is moved into the new component and returned. The newly created component can be obtained by using the parentComponent property of the MeshBody object.

methods.name: createForAssemblyContext
methods.description: Creates or returns a proxy for the native object - i.e. a new object that represents this object but adds the assembly context defined by the input occurrence. Fails if this object is not the NativeObject.

methods.name: cut
methods.description: Cuts the mesh body to the clipboard.

methods.name: findByTempId
methods.description: Returns the face group with the temporary id.

methods.name: moveToComponent
methods.description: Moves this mesh body from it's current component into the root component or the component owned by the specified occurrence.

properties.name: appearance
properties.description: Read-write property that gets and sets the current appearance of the body. Setting this property will result in applying an override appearance to the body and the AppearanceSourceType property will return OverrideAppearanceSource. Setting this property to null will remove any override.

properties.name: appearanceSourceType
properties.description: Read-write property that gets the source of the appearance for the body. If this returns OverrideAppearanceSource, an override exists on this body. The override can be removed by setting the Appearance property to null.

properties.name: area
properties.description: Returns the area in cm ^ 2.

properties.name: assemblyContext
properties.description: Returns the assembly occurrence (i.e. the occurrence) of this object in an assembly. This is only valid in the case where this is acting as a proxy in an assembly. Returns null in the case where the object is not in the context of an assembly but is already the native object.

properties.name: attributes
properties.description: Returns the collection of attributes associated with this mesh body.

properties.name: baseOrFormFeature
properties.description: This property returns the base or form feature that this mesh body is associated with. It returns null in a direct modeling design.

properties.name: boundingBox
properties.description: Returns the bounding box of this mesh body.

properties.name: displayMesh
properties.description: Returns the associated mesh that is used for the display. This will always be triangles and includes any textures.

properties.name: displayOverrides
properties.description: Gets the object that allows manipulation of overrides that control how the mesh is displayed in the interactive 3D view.

properties.name: entityToken
properties.description: Returns a token for the MeshBody object. This can be saved and used at a later time with the Design.findEntityByToken method to get back the same mesh body.   When using entity tokens it's important to understand that the token string returned for a specific entity can be different over time. However, even if you have two different token strings that were obtained from the same entity, when you use findEntityByToken they will both return the same entity. Because of that you should never compare entity tokens as way to determine what the token represents. Instead, you need to use the findEntityByToken method to get the two entities identified by the tokens and then compare them.

properties.name: faceGroups
properties.description: Returns a collection of all of the face groups in the body.

properties.name: isClosed
properties.description: Check to see if the mesh is closed - i.e. contains no edges with only one triangle. Returns true if the mesh is closed, false if not.

properties.name: isLightBulbOn
properties.description: Is the light bulb (as displayed in the browser) on. A mesh body will only be visible if the light bulb is switched on. However, the light bulb can be on and the mesh body is still invisible if the light bulb for all bodies or the owning component is off.

properties.name: isOriented
properties.description: Check to see if the mesh is oriented - i.e. every edge has at most two triangles, and those triangles have consistent orientations. Returns true if the mesh is oriented, false if not.

properties.name: isSelectable
properties.description: Gets and sets if the mesh body is selectable in the graphics window.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: isVisible
properties.description: Gets if the mesh body point is visible.

properties.name: material
properties.description: Gets and sets the physical material assigned to this mesh body.

properties.name: mesh
properties.description: Returns the original mesh data that was imported. This can include triangles, quads, and polygons.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Gets and sets the name of the mesh body as displayed in the browser.

properties.name: nativeObject
properties.description: The NativeObject is the object outside the context of an assembly and in the context of it's parent component. Returns null in the case where this object is not in the context of an assembly but is already the native object.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: opacity
properties.description: Gets and sets the opacity override assigned to this body. A value of 1.0 specifies that is it completely opaque and a value of 0.0 specifies that is it completely transparent.   This value is not necessarily related to what the user sees because the opacity is inherited. For example, if you this body is in a component and that component's opacity is set to something other than 1.0, the body will also be shown as slightly transparent even though the opacity property for the body will return 1.0. Because the component that contains the body can be referenced as an occurrence in other components and they can have different opacity settings, it's possible that different instances of the same body can display using different opacity levels. To get the opacity that it is being displayed with use the MeshBody.visibleOpacity property.   This is the API equivalent of the "Opacity Control" command available for the body in the browser.

properties.name: orientedMinimumBoundingBox
properties.description: Returns an oriented bounding box of the body that is best oriented to tightly fit the body.

properties.name: parentComponent
properties.description: Returns the parent Component.

properties.name: textureMapControl
properties.description: Returns the TextureMapControl object associated with this body when there is an appearance assigned to the body that has a texture associated with it. If there isn't a texture, this property will return null. If there is a texture, you can use the returned object to query and modify how the texture is applied to the body.

properties.name: visibleOpacity
properties.description: The user can set an override opacity for components and bodies these opacity overrides combine if children and parent components have overrides. This property returns the actual opacity that is being used to render the body. To set the opacity use the opacity property of the MeshBody object.

properties.name: volume
properties.description: Returns the volume in cm ^ 3. Returns 0 in the case the mesh body is not closed.

appearance.title: MeshBody.appearance Property
appearance.description: Read-write property that gets and sets the current appearance of the body. Setting this property will result in applying an override appearance to the body and the AppearanceSourceType property will return OverrideAppearanceSource. Setting this property to null will remove any override.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = meshBody_var.appearance

# Set the value of the property.
meshBody_var.appearance = propertyValue

appearanceSourceType.title: MeshBody.appearanceSourceType Property
appearanceSourceType.description: Read-write property that gets the source of the appearance for the body. If this returns OverrideAppearanceSource, an override exists on this body. The override can be removed by setting the Appearance property to null.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = meshBody_var.appearanceSourceType

# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = meshBody_var.area

assemblyContext.title: MeshBody.assemblyContext Property
assemblyContext.description: Returns the assembly occurrence (i.e. the occurrence) of this object in an assembly. This is only valid in the case where this is acting as a proxy in an assembly. Returns null in the case where the object is not in the context of an assembly but is already the native object.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = meshBody_var.assemblyContext

opacity.title: MeshBody.opacity Property
opacity.description: Gets and sets the opacity override assigned to this body. A value of 1.0 specifies that is it completely opaque and a value of 0.0 specifies that is it completely transparent. This value is not necessarily related to what the user sees because the opacity is inherited. For example, if you this body is in a component and that component's opacity is set to something other than 1.0, the body will also be shown as slightly transparent even though the opacity property for the body will return 1.0. Because the component that contains the body can be referenced as an occurrence in other components and they can have different opacity settings, it's possible that different instances of the same body can display using different opacity levels. To get the opacity that it is being displayed with use the MeshBody.visibleOpacity property. This is the API equivalent of the "Opacity Control" command available for the body in the browser.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = meshBody_var.opacity

# Set the value of the property.
meshBody_var.opacity = propertyValue

classType.title: MeshBody.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

syntax.python: returnValue = adsk.fusion.MeshBody.classType()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = adsk::fusion::MeshBody::classType();

classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

copy.title: MeshBody.copy Method
copy.description: Copies the mesh body to the clipboard.

syntax.python: returnValue = meshBody_var.copy()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = meshBody_var->copy();

copy.return_description: Returns true if the copy was successful.

copyToComponent.title: MeshBody.copyToComponent Method
copyToComponent.description: Creates a copy of this mesh body into the specified target.

syntax.python: returnValue = meshBody_var.copyToComponent(target)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = meshBody_var->copyToComponent(target);

copyToComponent.return_description: Returns the moved mesh body or null in the case the move failed.

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: The target can be either the root component or an occurrence.   In the case where an occurrence is specified, the mesh body will be copied into the parent component of the target occurrence and the target occurrence defines the transform of how the mesh body will be copied so that the body maintains it's same position with respect to the assembly.

createComponent.title: MeshBody.createComponent Method
createComponent.description: Creates a new component and occurrence within the component that currently owns this body. This body is moved into the new component and returned. The newly created component can be obtained by using the parentComponent property of the MeshBody object.

syntax.python: returnValue = meshBody_var.createComponent()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = meshBody_var->createComponent();

createComponent.return_description: Returns the MeshBody in the new component or null in the case the creation failed.

createForAssemblyContext.title: MeshBody.createForAssemblyContext Method
createForAssemblyContext.description: Creates or returns a proxy for the native object - i.e. a new object that represents this object but adds the assembly context defined by the input occurrence. Fails if this object is not the NativeObject.

syntax.python: returnValue = meshBody_var.createForAssemblyContext(occurrence)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = meshBody_var->createForAssemblyContext(occurrence);

createForAssemblyContext.return_description: Returns the proxy for the occurrence in the context of the specified occurrence. Returns null if it failed.

parameters.name: occurrence
parameters.description: The occurrence that represents the context you want to create this proxy in.

cut.title: MeshBody.cut Method
cut.description: Cuts the mesh body to the clipboard.

syntax.python: returnValue = meshBody_var.cut()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = meshBody_var->cut();

cut.return_description: Returns true if the cut was successful.

deleteMe.title: MeshBody.deleteMe Method

syntax.python: returnValue = meshBody_var.deleteMe()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = meshBody_var->deleteMe();

deleteMe.return_description: Returns true in the case where the selection was successful.

findByTempId.title: MeshBody.findByTempId Method
findByTempId.description: Returns the face group with the temporary id.

syntax.python: returnValue = meshBody_var.findByTempId(tempId)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = meshBody_var->findByTempId(tempId);

findByTempId.return_description: Returns the face group with the given tempId.

parameters.name: tempId
parameters.description: The ID of the face group to find.

moveToComponent.title: MeshBody.moveToComponent Method
moveToComponent.description: Moves this mesh body from it's current component into the root component or the component owned by the specified occurrence.

syntax.python: returnValue = meshBody_var.moveToComponent(target)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = meshBody_var->moveToComponent(target);

moveToComponent.return_description: Returns the moved mesh body or null in the case the move failed.

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: The target can be either the root component or an occurrence.   In the case where an occurrence is specified, the mesh body will be moved into the parent component of the target occurrence and the target occurrence defines the transform of how the mesh body will be copied so that the body maintains it's same position with respect to the assembly.
